queries: cloud sync API key bearer authentication
config set command telemetry opt out

build_from.reason: This is the file the epic explicitly names for 'conditionally bypass scrubField for Persona IDs, replacing them with cryptographic hashes'. It already owns the only anonymization/allowlist boundary in the codebase (PublicRecord, scrubField, ScrubPublicRecord, AnonymizeRecord) and is covered by prior clarifications establishing that scrubField's identity-merging behavior is by-design, not a bug.
build_from.suggested_approach: Do not bypass or weaken scrubField itself (it is the backstop for the existing public leaderboard export path, per docs/scorecard.md Privacy Model + clarifications-15.1_leaderboard-cost-na-rendering-Q3). Instead add a separate, explicitly-named hashing path (e.g. HashPersonaID / a telemetry-specific PublicRecord variant) so the Epic 10.0 leaderboard export allowlist is untouched and the new telemetry/cloud-sync payload is a distinct schema with its own allowlist.

semantic_matches.relevance: The exact function the epic's Payload Formatting task (T5) says to conditionally bypass. Its doc comment states it is defense-in-depth over the PublicRecord allowlist -- bypassing it for telemetry needs a new, separate allowlist rather than a flag on this function.

The existing public submission schema (Epic 10.0). It is a strict allowlist by design comment ('anything not here ... cannot leak'); the new cloud-sync payload (time/credits saved metrics, hashed persona) is a materially different shape and should not be grafted onto this struct.
Shows the established pattern for the single anonymization ingestion point -- any new Persona ID hashing path should follow the same 'scrub/hash once, at ingestion' shape.
Existing pattern for reading a strict env var override (LOG_LEVEL) at root-command construction time -- the model for ATCR_TELEMETRY=0 and ATCR_API_KEY reads.

Existing pointer-based 'nil = not set' precedence pattern for CLI flag overrides layered over config/registry tiers -- a natural model for how --sync-cloud and its API key should be threaded through, if the plan wants config-file/env/flag precedence.

Existing shared flag-registration helper in the file the epic names for --sync-cloud; it currently holds review-range flags and a PreRunE chaining pattern. The new addSyncCloudFlags helper should live here alongside it.
One of the two natural run-completion hooks where a usage ping would fire (after scorecard.EmitForReconcile). The telemetry call must be fire-and-forget and must not block or fail this function.

The other natural run-completion hook where a usage ping would fire (after a successful review or one-shot reconcile/verify/debate chain). The telemetry call must be fire-and-forget and must not block or fail this function.
The on-disk scorecard record schema. The new Persona ID hashing path and the --sync-cloud payload will consume fields from this record, but must not leak the raw Reviewer/Model values.
The existing public export path that calls scorecard.Export. A new telemetry/cloud-sync payload should be a separate function/path, not a modification of this public-leaderboard path.
Build version surfaced in telemetry payloads and cloud-sync requests the same way it is already stamped on the public submission envelope.
Another example of a shared flag-registration helper that attaches a fixed flag set to a *cobra.Command and is called from subcommand constructors.
The shared bridge that builds scorecard records for a reconcile run. Any --sync-cloud push that wants the reconciled run's data will need the same inputs (reviewers, findings, verification path).
Documents the existing allowlist/scrubbing privacy guarantee; must be updated to describe the new hashed-persona telemetry/cloud-sync path so docs and code stay in sync.
Enforces that every CLI flag and documented env var stays in sync; --sync-cloud, ATCR_TELEMETRY, and ATCR_API_KEY will need matching doc coverage.

existing_patterns.name: Allowlist-based public export schema
existing_patterns.description: internal/scorecard/export.go defines PublicRecord/ExportEnvelope as an explicit allowlist struct; only listed fields are ever serialized, so anything not added cannot leak. Persona/model are scrubbed exactly once at ingestion (Export/AnonymizeRecord), and finalize() never re-scrubs.
existing_patterns.follow_for: Any new telemetry/cloud-sync payload struct -- define it as its own allowlist type, not a superset of PublicRecord, and hash/anonymize once at construction.

existing_patterns.name: Strict opt-out env var + doc convention
existing_patterns.description: ATCR_DISABLE_AST_GROUPING is an existing precedent for a boolean-shaped ATCR_-prefixed opt-out env var, documented and asserted against in cmd/atcr/docs_audit_test.go (canonicalFlags/env-var coverage check).
existing_patterns.follow_for: ATCR_TELEMETRY=0 should follow the same naming/documentation/test-coverage convention so docs_audit's flag/env coverage check does not flag it as undocumented.

existing_patterns.name: cobra flag registration helpers
existing_patterns.description: Shared flag-registration helper functions attach a fixed flag set to a *cobra.Command and are called from each subcommand constructor. Examples: addSourceFlags in cmd/atcr/debt.go, addAutoFixFlags in cmd/atcr/autofix.go, and addRangeFlags in cmd/atcr/flags.go.
existing_patterns.follow_for: Registering --sync-cloud consistently across whichever command(s) it applies to (likely reconcile and the one-shot review path), instead of duplicating flag definitions inline.

existing_patterns.name: Env var read at root command construction
existing_patterns.description: cmd/atcr/main.go reads LOG_LEVEL via os.Getenv at newRootCmd() time and applies a trimmed, validated value.
existing_patterns.follow_for: Reading ATCR_TELEMETRY and ATCR_API_KEY the same way -- trimmed, validated, with a documented default.

existing_patterns.name: Fire-and-forget best-effort side effects
existing_patterns.description: cmd/atcr/reconcile.go and cmd/atcr/review.go perform non-fatal side effects (scorecard.EmitForReconcile, persistLocalDebt, recordHistory, writeAuditRecord) that log on failure and never change the command's exit code.
existing_patterns.follow_for: Telemetry usage pings and --sync-cloud pushes should be invoked the same way at run-completion: fire-and-forget, never block, never fail the parent command.

existing_patterns.name: Coded exit-code semantics
existing_patterns.description: cmd/atcr/main.go defines exitFailure=1, exitUsage=2, and a codedError type so subcommands can return distinct exit codes through errors.As.
existing_patterns.follow_for: The missing/invalid ATCR_API_KEY auth failure for --sync-cloud should use a new dedicated codedError constant (e.g. exitAuth=3) rather than the generic exitUsage code.

related_files.purpose: Owns PublicRecord allowlist, scrubField, AnonymizeRecord/ScrubPublicRecord -- the anonymization boundary the epic's T5 explicitly targets.
Documents the existing Privacy Model / allowlist guarantees for scorecard export; any new hashed-persona telemetry path changes what is user-visible truth here.
Root command construction and existing env-var-read pattern (LOG_LEVEL); natural home for ATCR_TELEMETRY / ATCR_API_KEY wiring and telemetry client lifecycle hook.
Run-completion path for the review command and one-shot review->reconcile->verify->debate chain; natural place to fire a usage telemetry ping and/or --sync-cloud push.
Run-completion path for the standalone reconcile command; natural place to fire a usage telemetry ping and/or --sync-cloud push.
Already exists and holds the shared addRangeFlags helper; the natural home for the new addSyncCloudFlags helper the epic names.
Defines the Record schema and Emit() used by both the local store and the export paths; --sync-cloud payload construction will read these fields.
Calls scorecard.Export for the public leaderboard; shows the existing export call site and helps keep the new telemetry/cloud-sync path separate.
Build version constant stamped into public submissions and telemetry payloads.
Shows the established CLIOverrides / tiered-precedence pattern already used for other CLI-settable behavior.
Example of a shared addXFlags() helper + subcommand structure for registering related flags consistently.
Example of a shared addAutoFixFlags() helper and PreRunE chaining pattern.

Enforces that CLI flags and documented env vars stay in sync (canonicalFlags coverage check) -- new --sync-cloud flag and ATCR_TELEMETRY/ATCR_API_KEY env vars will need matching doc coverage or this test fails.
Documentation index consumed by the website build; docs/telemetry.md must be added here or docs_audit_test.go's TestDocsIndexCoversEveryDoc fails.

integration_points.description: Best place to initialize a package-level telemetry client (or no-op client when ATCR_TELEMETRY=0 or ATCR_API_KEY absent) once, at process start, following the existing LOG_LEVEL-read pattern.
Natural place to add a parallel hashed-persona derivation for the Persona Leaderboard, mirroring the existing single-ingestion-point scrub pattern rather than modifying scrubField's behavior.

Where a `{event, lang, lines, status}` usage ping and a `--sync-cloud` push would fire on review completion -- needs a fire-and-forget, network-failure-safe call so it cannot block or fail CLI execution (AC1).

Where a `{event, lang, lines, status}` usage ping and a `--sync-cloud` push would fire on standalone reconcile completion -- needs a fire-and-forget, network-failure-safe call so it cannot block or fail CLI execution (AC1).
The new addSyncCloudFlags helper should be added here, following the same shared-helper shape and PreRunE chaining convention used by addRangeFlags.
The missing/invalid ATCR_API_KEY auth failure from --sync-cloud should return a new dedicated codedError (e.g. exitAuth=3) through this central exit-code mapping, not the generic exitUsage=2 code.

reusable_components.name: CLIOverrides pointer-precedence pattern
reusable_components.usage_example: A *string/*bool field per CLI-settable telemetry setting, resolved through the same nil-means-unset precedence chain used for PayloadMode/TimeoutSecs/etc.

reusable_components.name: addSourceFlags-style flag helper
reusable_components.usage_example: An addSyncCloudFlags(cmd) helper registering --sync-cloud (and any accompanying flags) once, called from whichever subcommand(s) need it.

reusable_components.name: addRangeFlags helper + PreRunE chaining
reusable_components.usage_example: The new addSyncCloudFlags helper can live in the same file and follow the same PreRunE chaining pattern if it needs cross-flag validation.

reusable_components.name: Record / EmitInput schema
reusable_components.usage_example: A --sync-cloud payload builder can accept scorecard.Record values (or EmitInput) and map them to a separate allowlist type rather than extending Record itself.

architecture_notes: internal/scorecard/export.go's scrubField/PublicRecord allowlist is an explicitly by-design privacy boundary (confirmed in docs/scorecard.md and prior clarifications, e.g. clarifications-15.1_leaderboard-cost-na-rendering-Q3) for the existing Epic 10.0 public leaderboard export. The epic's T5 instruction to 'conditionally bypass scrubField ... replacing them with cryptographic hashes when telemetry is active' should NOT be implemented as a runtime toggle on the existing scrubField/PublicRecord path -- that would weaken a documented privacy guarantee for the unrelated leaderboard-export feature. The design should introduce a separate, clearly-named allowlist/schema for the telemetry and --sync-cloud payloads, reusing the 'scrub/hash once at ingestion' shape but not the same code path or struct.

No existing package handles background/fire-and-forget network calls anywhere in the codebase (semantic search for 'background non-blocking HTTP request' returned no real matches) -- internal/telemetry will be new from scratch, not an extension of an existing client. It must be goroutine-based with a bounded timeout and must never block or fail the parent CLI command (per AC1's fail-open requirement) -- follow the 'Panic Safety' and 'Defer Cleanup' guidance in implementation-standards.md for goroutines.

cmd/atcr/docs_audit_test.go enforces that every CLI flag and documented env var stays in sync via a canonicalFlags coverage check -- --sync-cloud, ATCR_TELEMETRY, and ATCR_API_KEY will need corresponding documentation entries or this existing test will fail.
cmd/atcr/flags.go already exists (it currently holds addRangeFlags), so the epic's listed 'cmd/atcr/flags.go' component is a file to modify/extend, not a file to create from scratch.

There is currently no `atcr config` command in the command tree (main.go's AddCommand list does not include one). The epic's opt-out surface 'atcr config set telemetry false' therefore requires creating a new cmd/atcr/config.go with a `config set` subcommand, or the opt-out must be implemented via the env var alone. The codebase-discovery file flags this as an integration gap.

Run-completion telemetry/sync hooks must be added to both cmd/atcr/review.go:runReview and cmd/atcr/reconcile.go:runReconcile, because either command can be the final user-facing command that produces scorecard data. Both paths already perform best-effort side effects that log on failure and never change exit codes, which is the exact pattern the telemetry/sync call should follow.

files_to_modify.reason: Add Persona ID hashing support for the Persona Leaderboard export path without weakening the existing scrubField/PublicRecord allowlist used by the Epic 10.0 leaderboard export.

Wire ATCR_TELEMETRY env var read and telemetry client initialization at root command construction, following the existing LOG_LEVEL pattern. Also add a dedicated exit-code constant for --sync-cloud auth failures.
Add the shared addSyncCloudFlags helper (and any related telemetry flags) in the file the epic explicitly names; it already holds addRangeFlags.
Fire the usage telemetry ping and/or --sync-cloud push on review completion in a fire-and-forget, non-blocking, non-failing way.
Fire the usage telemetry ping and/or --sync-cloud push on standalone reconcile completion in a fire-and-forget, non-blocking, non-failing way.
Document the new Persona ID hashing behavior and telemetry/cloud-sync privacy model so docs and code stay accurate.
Add docs/telemetry.md to the documentation index so the website build and docs_audit_test.go's index-coverage test stay in sync.

files_to_create.purpose: Background, fail-open anonymous usage telemetry client (the NEW package named in the epic's Components Touched).
Implements the new `atcr config set telemetry false` opt-out command surface named in the epic; there is currently no `config` command in the CLI.
Privacy policy and opt-out instructions for telemetry (ATCR_TELEMETRY=0, atcr config set telemetry false), named in the epic's Components Touched (docs/).

integration_gaps.impact: The epic's opt-out surface `atcr config set telemetry false` cannot be implemented without first creating a config command group and `set` subcommand.
integration_gaps.recommendation: Create cmd/atcr/config.go with `atcr config set <key> <value>` (scoped to telemetry for now) and register it in main.go's AddCommand list.

integration_gaps.gap: No existing background HTTP client package
integration_gaps.impact: internal/telemetry must be built from scratch; there is no shared fire-and-forget HTTP client to extend.
integration_gaps.recommendation: Build internal/telemetry as a small, self-contained package with a bounded-timeout goroutine, panic recovery, and a no-op implementation when disabled.

integration_gaps.impact: A missing/invalid ATCR_API_KEY would currently map to the generic exitUsage=2 code, making it indistinguishable from other configuration errors in scripts/CI.
integration_gaps.recommendation: Add a new exitAuth constant (e.g. 3) and return a codedError with that code from the --sync-cloud validation path.

integration_gaps.gap: Run-completion telemetry hook spans two commands
integration_gaps.impact: The usage ping and --sync-cloud push must fire from both final commands to avoid under-reporting when users run reconcile directly.
integration_gaps.recommendation: Add a small shared helper in cmd/atcr (e.g. run_helpers.go) that both runReview and runReconcile call with the run metadata.

integration_gaps.impact: Adding a new docs/*.md file without linking it in docs/README.md will fail docs_audit_test.go's TestDocsIndexCoversEveryDoc.
integration_gaps.recommendation: Update docs/README.md to link docs/telemetry.md under the observability section.

test_patterns.framework: go test (standard testing package, testify assert/require where used)
test_patterns.test_location: same package directory as code under test, e.g. internal/scorecard/export_test.go and internal/telemetry/client_test.go
